skill.name: build-diagnostics
skill.description: Diagnoses build, typecheck, and test blockers through reproduction, context gathering, root-cause analysis, and verified fixes.
skill.summary: Guide systematic diagnosis and repair of build blockers.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick and weak-crypto patterns, but review found they are markdown code fences and inline command examples, not executable Ruby or cryptographic code. The confirmed risk is operational: the skill instructs assistants to run local build commands, inspect files, use web search, and implement fixes, which is legitimate for diagnostics but requires user oversight.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill explicitly tells the assistant to use Bash and run project commands such as build, typecheck, and test commands. This is expected for a build diagnostics skill, but it can execute package scripts from an untrusted repository if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source directly instructs Bash usage and named npm commands. Confidence is high for operational command-execution risk, but no malicious command or payload is present.

medium_findings.title: Repository Modification Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to implement minimal fixes and gives an example of creating directories with fs.mkdir recursive. This is legitimate repair guidance, but it can alter repository files or generated paths if applied without user approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains direct instructions to implement fixes and a filesystem-change example. The risk is moderated because this is prose guidance, not executable code.

low_findings.title: External Research May Expose Project Details
low_findings.description: The workflow recommends MCP documentation lookup and web search for known issues. Users should avoid sending private logs, secrets, proprietary paths, or customer data to external search or documentation tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source explicitly mentions web search and reference documentation. No exfiltration endpoint or instruction to send secrets was found, so this is a privacy caution rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static high findings for weak cryptographic algorithms do not correspond to cryptographic code in the reviewed skill. The cited lines are frontmatter or diagnostic templates, and no hashing or encryption implementation appears in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review found ordinary markdown text at the flagged locations. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Command Examples Triggered Static Command Patterns
dangerous_patterns.description: The many Ruby backtick execution alerts are false positives caused by markdown fences and inline command examples. The file is still relevant to command risk because it instructs an assistant to run local diagnostic commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears in markdown examples, not Ruby code. The semantic command-execution behavior is still clearly documented in the skill instructions.

content.value_statement: Build failures often hide their true cause behind noisy logs. This skill guides systematic reproduction, context gathering, root-cause analysis, and verified fixes.
actual_capabilities: Guides exact reproduction of build, typecheck, and test failures.
Structures context gathering across logs, configuration files, and related code.
Separates visible symptoms from likely root causes before proposing fixes.
Provides confidence levels, validation plans, and rollback considerations.
Includes common patterns for memory errors, missing directories, type mismatches, and circular dependencies.
Defines escalation criteria when the diagnosis cannot be verified.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automated diagnostic tooling.
Requires access to the target repository and build environment.
May run project commands that execute package scripts from the repository.
Depends on the assistant and user to avoid sharing private logs through web search.

use_cases.title: Repair CI Build Failures
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to reproduce failing build steps, inspect environment differences, and define a verified fix for CI pipelines.

use_cases.title: Resolve Typecheck Blockers
use_cases.description: Trace TypeScript errors from failing command output to interfaces, call sites, and compatible examples already in the codebase.

use_cases.title: Investigate Test Execution Problems
use_cases.description: Review test structure, run targeted failures, compare working examples, and document why tests are missing or failing.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose a Build Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use build-diagnostics on this build failure. Reproduce the command, collect the relevant logs and config files, then explain the likely root cause before changing files.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate Type Errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Use build-diagnostics to analyze these typecheck errors. Group related errors, inspect the type definitions and call sites, and propose the smallest verified fix.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Fix Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use build-diagnostics to produce a fix plan for this blocker. Include the root cause, confidence level, risks, alternatives, validation steps, and rollback plan.

prompt_templates.title: Handle a Complex CI Blocker
prompt_templates.prompt: Use build-diagnostics for this recurring CI blocker. Compare local and CI behavior, inspect config and dependency changes, test a minimal fix, and escalate if verification is impossible.

output_examples.input: A Next.js build fails with a Turbopack manifest write error.
output_examples.output: The report identifies the exact failing command, relevant Next.js configuration, filesystem state, probable missing directory cause, confidence level, fix strategy, and validation command.

output_examples.input: A TypeScript check reports many assignment errors after an API change.
output_examples.output: The response groups related errors, names the changed interface, lists affected call sites, recommends either interface adjustment or value mapping, and defines success criteria.

output_examples.input: Tests are expected but no meaningful tests were added.
output_examples.output: The diagnosis explains whether scope, missing examples, or tooling blocked test creation, then provides next steps and a clear escalation question if needed.

best_practices: Run diagnostic commands in a controlled repository and review package scripts first.
Collect complete error output before editing files or proposing a fix.
Verify every fix with the same command that originally failed.
anti_patterns: Do not make speculative changes before reproducing the blocker.
Do not paste private logs or secrets into external search tools.
Do not claim success when the failing command has not been rerun.

faq.question: What does this skill help diagnose?
faq.answer: It helps diagnose build failures, typecheck errors, test issues, and other blockers that need root-cause analysis.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: The skill gives instructions to run commands, but execution depends on the assistant tool and user permissions.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: It can be used with private repositories, but users should review commands and avoid sending private logs to external tools.

faq.question: Can it fix files?
faq.answer: It guides minimal file changes after diagnosis, but every change should be reviewed and verified locally.

faq.question: What tools does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects shell access, file reading, code search, optional browser testing, and optional documentation lookup.

faq.question: When should the assistant escalate?
faq.answer: It should escalate when it cannot reproduce the issue, cannot verify a fix, or the fix requires major architecture changes.
